name: Recent review emphasizes schedule, delivery, and safety pharmacology barriers
summary: Li and Tay 2026 supports the current translation boundary: partial reprogramming is a plausible rejuvenation strategy, but the review frames schedule, delivery, and safety pharmacology as de-risking requirements for early clinical entry.
statement: A 2026 review presents partial reprogramming as a therapeutic strategy for aging and disease while proposing a translational checklist centered on schedule, delivery, and safety pharmacology before clinical claims can be strengthened.
population_or_model: Review of partial-reprogramming and epigenetic-rejuvenation evidence across model systems and disease areas
quantitative_note: Narrative review; no pooled effect size.
caveats: This is a review, not a new interventional result.
The review is useful for boundary mapping and source triangulation but does not establish human efficacy.
Commercial and disease-specific activity still needs source-level registry and outcome checks.
